description: Fonoran grammar particle inventory. Particles mark relationships in the sentence skeleton (Subject · Time · Event · Object · Modifiers); they are invariant and never fuse into lexical spellings (docs/fonoran-grammar.md Rule 3). All forms are reserved in data/fonoran-primitive-roots-config.json so the root generator never assigns them to a lexical concept.
scope_note: This is a closed, minimal class. A particle belongs here ONLY if it is genuinely grammatical (cannot be expressed as a lexical concept) AND is either sanctioned by the grammar doc or actively wired in the translator. Spatial/relational meaning (in/at/toward/from/with, this/that) is LEXICAL in Fonoran — it lives in concepts/roots such as inside, here, there, near, far, up, down, reach — so those are deliberately NOT particles. Personal pronouns other than mi resolve lexically and are out of scope for now.
note: Present tense has no particle: it is the default when the Time slot is empty. Polarity is grammar, not vocabulary: false = no + true, different = no + same. Quantifier pronouns compose at the particle/root layer: nobody = no + person, everyone = all + person.
triggers_note: triggers[] are English surface words that compile to this particle. Empty triggers[] means the particle is core grammar emitted programmatically (tense via the translator's tense pass; the question marker when a wh-word is present).

particles.note: Omitted on surface. Default when Time slot is empty.

particles.gloss: question marker; flags an interrogative clause
only; restricts focus to one thing

quantifier_pronouns.note: Composed at the particle/root layer, not lexical. 'neg' -> logic_not particle; 'all'/'some' resolve to their lexical roots (concepts all/some).

deliberately_lexical.note: Handled by concepts/roots, NOT particles, to avoid duplicating the lexicon.
pronouns: you/we/they/he/she/it resolve lexically; only mi is a particle
connectives: and/or/but handled structurally via clause splitting
